title: Multi-monitor support: one taskbar per screen with per-screen windows

description: Fix CG↔AppKit coordinate conversion; extract to TaskbarraCor
details:
Create `Sources/TaskbarraCore/ScreenCoordinateConverter.swift`: a small pure type (or free functions) converting rects between AppKit space (bottom-left origin of primary display) and CG/AX global space (top-left origin of primary display). API takes the primary screen height explicitly (e.g. `init(primaryScreenHeight: CGFloat)`) so it is testable without AppKit. Conversion: `cgY = primaryScreenHeight - appKitRect.maxY`, x/size unchanged; the inverse is symmetric.

Replace `WorkAreaReservation.convertToWindowCoordinates` (Sources/Taskbarra/WorkAreaReservation.swift) with this converter, passing `NSScreen.screens.first?.frame.height`. The current formula `screenFrame.minY + (screenFrame.maxY - frame.maxY)` is only correct for the primary screen — this task fixes that latent bug.

Add `Tests/TaskbarraCoreTests/ScreenCoordinateConverterTests.swift` covering: primary-screen rect roundtrip, a secondary screen to the right (same y-range), a secondary screen ABOVE the primary (AppKit y > primary height → negative CG y), and roundtrip identity. Register the tests in `Tests/TaskbarraCoreTests/main.swift` following the existing pattern.

Verification gate: `swift build && swift test` exits 0 and new tests run (test output lists the new test names). STOP if `WorkAreaReservation`'s behavior on the primary screen changes (existing single-monitor behavior must be identical).
notes: Added TaskbarraCore ScreenCoordinateConverter with primary/right/above-display round-trip tests; WorkAreaReservation now uses the global primary-display flip. `swift build && swift test` passes: 39 tests, 0 failures.

details:
In `Sources/Taskbarra/TaskbarGeometry.swift` add `static func forScreen(_ screen: NSScreen, barHeight: CGFloat = Self.defaultHeight) -> TaskbarGeometry` using `screen.frame`. Keep `forMainScreen` as a thin wrapper (`NSScreen.main ?? NSScreen.screens.first`) for now — callers migrate in t-004. No behavior change for existing callers.

Verification gate: `swift build && swift test` exits 0.
notes: Added TaskbarGeometry.forScreen(_:) and retained forMainScreen as a compatibility wrapper. `swift build && swift test` passes: 39 tests, 0 failures.

description: Window→screen assignment logic in TaskbarraCore + tests
details:
Create `Sources/TaskbarraCore/WindowScreenAssigner.swift`: pure logic that, given `[WindowInfo]` and a list of screen descriptors `[(id: UInt32, cgFrame: CGRect)]` (CG global space; the caller converts NSScreen frames using ScreenCoordinateConverter from t-001), returns the display ID for each window. Rule: largest intersection area between `window.bounds` and each screen's cgFrame wins; ties break to the lower display ID for determinism; zero intersection → nil (caller maps nil to the primary/main bar). Windows spanning two displays (possible when 'Displays have separate Spaces' is off) are handled naturally by max-intersection.

Add `Tests/TaskbarraCoreTests/WindowScreenAssignerTests.swift`: window fully on screen A; window straddling A/B with 60/40 split → A; window off all screens (minimized stale bounds) → nil; exact 50/50 tie → deterministic. Register in `main.swift`.

Verification gate: `swift build && swift test` exits 0 with the new tests listed in output.
notes: Added DisplayDescriptor and deterministic WindowScreenAssigner using largest intersection area with lower-display-ID tie breaking; covered contained, straddling, off-screen, tie, and batch assignment cases. `swift build && swift test` passes: 44 tests, 0 failures.

description: Panel-per-screen refactor: coordinator + per-screen panel co
details:
Split `Sources/Taskbarra/TaskbarWindowController.swift` into:

1. `TaskbarPanelController` (new file): owns ONE `NSPanel` for ONE screen (identified by `CGDirectDisplayID`, resolving the current `NSScreen` by ID on each placement pass). Receives the shared `WindowStore`, `NotificationStore`, and an interaction controller from outside. Uses `TaskbarGeometry.forScreen(_:)`. Handles its own show/hide/reposition. Renders `TaskbarView` filtered to its display (filtering itself lands in t-007; until then each panel may show all windows — acceptable intermediate state).
2. `MultiMonitorTaskbarCoordinator` (new file): owns the SINGLE shared `WindowStore` + `NotificationStore` + `AXWindowWorkAreaCoordinator` + `RectangleCompatibilityCoordinator` + fullscreen polling, and a `[CGDirectDisplayID: TaskbarPanelController]` map. `reconcileScreens()`: enumerate `NSScreen.screens`, extract display IDs via `deviceDescription[NSDeviceDescriptionKey("NSScreenNumber")]`, SKIP mirrored screens (`CGDisplayIsInMirrorSet(id) && CGDisplayMirrorsDisplay(id) != kCGNullDirectDisplay`), create panels for new IDs, tear down panels for vanished IDs, reposition survivors. Trigger from `TaskbarPlacementObserver`, debounced ~250ms (cancel-and-restart `Task.sleep` pattern like existing polling code). Must be idempotent.
3. `AppCoordinator` (`Sources/Taskbarra/AppCoordinator.swift`): replace `TaskbarWindowController` usage with the coordinator; `showTaskbar()` creates/starts it, `showOnboarding()` tears it down.

Keep `TaskbarWindowConfigurator` unchanged and apply it to every panel. Keep the per-init structure such that `WindowStore.startMonitoring()`/`NotificationStore.startMonitoring()` are called exactly ONCE (coordinator level), not per panel. Delete `TaskbarWindowController` once nothing references it.

Verification gate: `swift build && swift test` exits 0; `grep -rn "TaskbarWindowController" Sources Tests` returns nothing; single-monitor behavior unchanged (one panel on the only screen). STOP if WindowStore monitoring would start more than once per app lifetime.
notes: Replaced the single TaskbarWindowController with MultiMonitorTaskbarCoordinator plus one TaskbarPanelController per non-mirrored CGDirectDisplayID. Monitoring stores start once, screen changes are debounced 250ms, hotplug reconciliation is idempotent, and AppCoordinator owns lifecycle. `swift build && swift test` passes: 44 tests, 0 failures; no TaskbarWindowController references remain.

description: Per-screen work-area reservation for maximized windows
details:
Depends on t-001 (correct conversion) and t-004 (coordinator owns work area).

Rework `WorkAreaReservation` (Sources/Taskbarra/WorkAreaReservation.swift) from a single global screen/usable frame into a per-display registry: `apply(geometry:for displayID:)` storing `(screenFrame, usableFrame)` per display in BOTH AppKit and CG space (via ScreenCoordinateConverter), plus `removeReservation(for:)` called when a panel is torn down. The coordinator applies one geometry per live screen during `reconcileScreens()`.

Rework `AXWindowWorkAreaCoordinator.reconcile(windows:)` (Sources/Taskbarra/AXWindowWorkAreaCoordinator.swift): for each window, resolve its display via `WindowScreenAssigner` (t-003) against the reserved displays' CG frames, then call `WindowFramePolicy.shouldMoveMaximizedWindow` with THAT display's `screenFrame`/`usableFrame` (CG space) — `WindowFramePolicy` in TaskbarraCore needs no change. Windows assigned to no display are skipped. `lastAppliedFramesByWindowID` bookkeeping stays as-is.

CRITICAL: the usableFrame passed to AX `setFrame` must belong to the same display the window was assigned to; a mismatch teleports user windows across monitors. If any per-display frame pairing logic is extractable as pure code, put it in TaskbarraCore with a test asserting assignment→frame pairing.

Verification gate: `swift build && swift test` exits 0. Manual check deferred to t-008. STOP if reconcile would ever apply display A's usableFrame to a window assigned to display B.
notes: WorkAreaReservation now stores CG-space screen/usable frames per display; AXWindowWorkAreaCoordinator resolves each window to the matching DisplayWorkArea before applying frames. Added pure WindowWorkAreaResolver tests proving secondary/straddling pairing and off-screen skip. `swift build && swift test` passes: 47 tests, 0 failures.

details:
Depends on t-004.

Replace the single frontmost-app fullscreen check (`frontmostApplicationHasFullscreenWindow` + `windowCoversMainScreen`, formerly in TaskbarWindowController, now living in the coordinator after t-004) with per-display occlusion:

1. Pure core: `Sources/TaskbarraCore/DisplayOcclusionPolicy.swift` — given layer-0 on-screen `[WindowInfo]` (already available from WindowStore; bounds are CG space) and `[(id, cgFrame)]` display descriptors, return the set of occluded display IDs: a display is occluded if any window's bounds enclose its cgFrame within tolerance 4pt. Unit tests in `Tests/TaskbarraCoreTests/DisplayOcclusionPolicyTests.swift` (registered in main.swift): fullscreen window occludes only its display; maximized-above-bar window (height = screen − 48) does NOT occlude; two displays, one fullscreen → only that ID.
2. Coordinator glue: on the existing 1s polling tick and on snapshot changes, compute occluded IDs and hide/show each panel individually. Keep the existing AX true-fullscreen check (`AXWindowResolver.isTrueFullscreen`) as an OR-condition for the frontmost app, mapped to the display the fullscreen window sits on (via WindowScreenAssigner).
3. Separate-Spaces rule: if `NSScreen.screensHaveSeparateSpaces == false` and ANY display is occluded, hide ALL panels (native fullscreen blanks other displays in that mode).

Verification gate: `swift build && swift test` exits 0 with new occlusion tests in output.
notes: Added pure DisplayOcclusionPolicy and tests for per-display fullscreen coverage, usable-area non-occlusion, dual display coverage, and ignored minimized/overlay windows. Coordinator now unions CG coverage with AX true-fullscreen assignments, hides only affected panels when Spaces are separate, and hides all when they are not. `swift build && swift test` passes: 51 tests, 0 failures.

description: Per-bar window filtering in the taskbar UI
details:
Depends on t-003 and t-004.

Compute window→display assignment once per snapshot at the store/coordinator level (NOT per panel): after `WindowStore.refreshPassiveSnapshot()` publishes, the coordinator (or a small observable wrapper) runs `WindowScreenAssigner` over the snapshot with current display descriptors and exposes `windowIDsByDisplay: [CGDirectDisplayID: [WindowInfo.ID]]` (order-preserving). Rules: window with no intersecting display (e.g. minimized with stale bounds that still resolve → use stale bounds; truly unresolvable → primary display's bar, where primary = `NSScreen.screens[0]`, NOT `NSScreen.main`).

Each `TaskbarPanelController`'s `TaskbarView` renders only its display's windows. Keep `TaskbarView` (Sources/Taskbarra/TaskbarView.swift) dumb: pass the filtered list (or a display-scoped view model) in; do not thread display IDs through WindowSnapshotButton. `activeWindowID` / `minimizedWindowIDs` / icons remain global lookups — a bar simply won't render IDs not in its list.

Verification gate: `swift build && swift test` exits 0. With one monitor, the bar must show the identical window set as before the refactor (manual sanity: run app, compare). STOP if assignment runs per-panel (N× cost) instead of once per snapshot.
notes: Added shared observable WindowDisplayStore; coordinator computes one assignment map per snapshot/topology change with NSScreen.screens[0] fallback, and each TaskbarView renders only its display-scoped IDs. Added unresolved-window fallback coverage. `swift build && swift test` passes: 52 tests, 0 failures; single-display assignment includes all intersecting windows plus minimized/off-screen fallback windows.

description: Docs update + manual multi-monitor verification checklist
details:
Depends on t-005, t-006, t-007.

1. Update `CONTEXT.md` decision table row "Monitores": replace "Una sola barra en el monitor principal" with the new behavior (una barra por monitor; cada barra muestra solo las ventanas de su pantalla; pantallas espejadas excluidas). Keep the table format and Spanish prose, one logical line, no hard wrapping.
2. Add `docs/adr/0003-multi-monitor-taskbars.md` following the style of the existing ADRs in `docs/adr/` (read 0001/0002 first for format): decision = per-screen bars with per-screen window filtering keyed by CGDirectDisplayID; note the CG vs AppKit coordinate trap and the screensHaveSeparateSpaces fullscreen rule; note rejected alternative (all windows on every bar) as possible future toggle.
3. Append a manual verification checklist to the ADR or a docs note, covering: hotplug (connect/disconnect external display → bars appear/disappear without restart), sleep/wake, mirrored mode (single bar), window dragged across displays moves between bars on next refresh, maximized window on secondary display respects that display's bar, fullscreen video on one display hides only that bar (separate Spaces ON) or all bars (OFF), Rectangle bottom-gap still applied.

Verification gate: `swift build && swift test` exits 0; both doc files exist and render as markdown (`ls docs/adr/0003-*` non-empty). Manual checklist execution requires multi-display hardware — mark this plan's manual portion for the user, do NOT claim hardware verification.
notes: Updated CONTEXT.md monitor decision, added ADR 0003 with coordinate/fullscreen rationale and an explicitly unclaimed multi-display hardware checklist, and removed stale TaskbarWindowController references from ADR 0002. Final `swift build && swift test` passes: 52 tests, 0 failures; `git diff --check` passes.
